item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [DELETED, DOUBLE POST]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I think that something related to SCP-231 or maybe even SCP-231-1 through 7 were found inside SCP-110, hence the heavy redaction on SCP-110's file and why SCP-231-7-1 is contained with Procedure 110-Montauk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should be a nexus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think that 110 is part of a stable time loop. Foundation finds subterranean city, builds city on top of it as cover. As time goes by the SCP below becomes less and less relevant while the city above starts being used to house personnel, eventually becoming a research site in its own right. Then something goes wrong, the city is temporally displaced, and the foundation discover it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea, but every [DATA EXPUNGED] makes me die a little inside. Expungement is the act of deleting data, documents, or media. Which means that when you [DATA EXPUNGED] the location, nobody who needs to know the location will know it. If you want to keep the data archived so that the people with proper clearance can still use it, you [REDACT] the data, or just ██████ it, which is what a redaction actually looks like when applied to a document.

I know the original author probably isn't around to do anything about it, but consider this my two cents if anyone decides it's time to revise the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm downvoting mostly on the account that SCP-1678 does this SCP better than this SCP. I'm in agreement with Joreth that this could use some cleaning up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm surprised that SCP-110 has exsited for so many years without documentation of SCP-110-01's details. I too want to know more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea behind this is solid, but the tone really didn't age well at all

item.title: Re: Future Tag?
item.content:encoded:
Wasn't too sure about adding it at first, but after

Information gained from SCP-110-01 and SCP-███ reveal that there has been a large temporal disturbance in the area of SCP-110

and

The earliest documents found claim that the city was built in [DATA EXPUNGED] by the [DATA EXPUNGED] group.

I've decided to add it.

item.title: Future Tag?
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this have the "future" tag? I was looking for this, and thought a search for "city, future" would return it, but it didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the risk of Necro, I agree. This has significant upvotes so maybe it should be left, but it feels overlong AND underdeveloped. I would love this to be a major cannon setting and love the idea. But this entry should be better than it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this concept, but the execution really hasn't aged well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
List of Notable SCPs found within SCP-110 [DATA EXPUNGED]

Oh 110, you little scamp! Such a tease.

Upvoting on grounds of it having inspiring many possibilities within my noggin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Once I get it formulated I'll post a draft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One may find the posting history of an article through the History tab at the bottom.

That being said, this article is by a "far2", who was an Old Member that brought a bunch of SCP articles over from the Old Site. It's likely that 110 has no official author. Go for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this article, and I've got a fun idea about a tale featuring SCP-110-1 (I think it's fun, anyway). Do we know who the author is so I can discuss this with them and avoid stepping on toes? I want to make sure before I let this one out from under my hat…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree there should be some more information about SCP 110-01. If it was able to greet them, somebody must have asked it some questions later.

Also a little re-wording might be good for this: "SCP-Research Ring: This city apparently had its outermost ring devoted to research of Safe and Euclid class SCPs, although no documentation within the city is able to confirm this ring's existence." I thought that meant the ring itself was lost and they couldn't find it, but I guess what it really means is that they've found no documents mentioning it. Maybe that's nit-picky, but a little re-wording would make that much clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it should be revealed what SCP 110-01 is, and maybe some of the ones found inside the city. I mean, it's been so long and the article just casually mentions 110-01 and says it's detained for questioning without mentioning it again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That bugged the crap out of me. SCP-110-01 is strongly implied to be sentient; he/she is referred to as a "who," not a "what," and is subsequently "detained." Even if we're to assume that further details pertaining to him/her were [REDACTED], the existence of a sentient being who was able to provide information should change the whole direction of the report. After the initial mention of him/her, all other information seems to have come from "maps" and "records." Are we to understand that SCP-110-01 doesn't know whether or not manufacturing took place anywhere in the city? That he/she didn't even know where the "rich part of town" was? I can only assume that he/she was either very, very young (which makes the implied "sole survivor" scenario unlikely), or wasn't around to answer questions very long.

Or maybe…SCP-110-01 was one of the escaped SCP's from the outer ring? That could be a hell of a story.

item.title: Re: Escape?
item.content:encoded:
You're in a city. Suddenly EVERYTHING goes wrong. Power and life support goes out. Some areas collapse in on themselves as you're suddenly underground. To make matters worse, you're a Foundation site and things are trying to get out and at you.

They probably didn't realize they were "only" a third of a mile underground. It might not have done them any good if they did.

Because SCP-110-01 survived and greeted the Foundation when they showed up. And EVERYONE else was dead.

Honestly, this sounds like a better plot then many of the SyFy movies… But everyone still dies in the end.

item.title: Re: Escape?
item.content:encoded: This SCP reminds me of a book called The City of Ember. In that book, the people in the self-contained underground city didn't even know that there was a surface world. Maybe something similar happened here?

item.title: Escape?
item.content:encoded:
I'm intrigued by the idea that so many people died. You'd think there'd be a mass exodus, right? Why stay in a self-contained city that's dangerous when people could escape to the surface?
That opens up a thought: Were the inhabitants prevented from escaping by authority or fear of what is above ground?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And that it greeted the explorers. Something sentient, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's never any mention of SCP-110-01 other then that it exists…

item.title: replying to an old post
item.content:encoded: No, there is no canon. But at the time, gears was using the article for something, seen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely sounds like a Euclid class to me. I don't think our classification system actually has gaps that "unclassified" SCPs would fall under.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Questions from the chat, from a user (Taveena) who has not yet been accepted:
"Shouldn't SCP-110 be reclassified as at least Euclid, if SCPs have been found there?" (How is it even "Unclassifiable"?)
"Also, shouldn't the list of SCPs found there be expunged or something instead of 'to be added'?"

item.title: Hmmm
item.content:encoded: I like this concept. Is it possible to add to it, logs and the like, maybe a few of the SCPs contained there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He can't touch because, despite people always saying there's no canon, this is canon.

Plus, it's one of those old SCPs that most people feel are immune to revision or deletion.

I like to think of it as the 'catch-all' for anyone else trying to write an underground/undersea city.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wait, why can't he touch?

worst comes to worst, we revert it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're in a mine, you tend to need some sort of life support, even if it isn't as extensive as a SCUBA or the ISS's. There's no air underground.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point taken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*cough*Yes it is*cough*

Just without water.

I'll clean it up a bit.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: *cough*no it isn't*cough*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *cough* BioShock*cough*

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
Trust me, at the time, the life support was VERY needed…

If i can rouse Kain up, we can get a chapter compiled, and maybe explain a bit more…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why would the inhabitants of a city absolutely NEED a life support system? Unless it was underwater, in space, or from another planet I can't see why losing it would kill everyone.

And at Dr. Gears, I read that story. It's fucking amazing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kain and I are working on a big story with this one. While i like your ideas, just give us a little time with it before making any big changes. If you're interested in the story, see The Lockdown.

item.title: Re: Good, but could be shorter
item.content:encoded: Well, this one came over from the old site, so it's likely the author is not around anymore. Read the 'how to' page, and then feel free to give it a try.

item.title: Good, but could be shorter
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea. I think it'd be an interesting setting, and should come with a bunch of found documents and investigation logs in the library. But I think the actual SCP entry should be shorter.
I don't always agree that less is more, but it really applies to this one. I think this article could be significantly improved by making the Special Containment Procedure section longer (hiding an entire city, with potential temporal disturbances, and unknown, uncontained SCP objects in the vicinity? It should be significant), and the description shorter. Leave little details like the geography, architecture, and function for other documents.
A list of objects found within would be good, so long as it's appropriately vague and tantalizing.
